content: Autonomous curious ICM-driven agents (from Topic A) can act as self-supervised 'world model curators' that grow their memory by proactively mining high-value unlabeled environment data to fine-tune 2025 self-supervised world models (from Topic B), creating a recursive closed-loop improvement cycle where agent curiosity drives world model accuracy, and more accurate world models in turn enable more targeted curious exploration. This creates a system that does not require manually labeled training data to continuously improve both the agent's policy and its internal world representation.
